77	where
78	    F: FnOnce(&mut rbtdri_Context) -> rbtdre_Verdict,
79	{

91	/// Setup hook for crucible fixtures: charge → CrucibleActive assertion.
92	/// Reads ctx from thread-local. Returns Err on any step failure; engine
93	/// surfaces this as a fixture failure and runs teardown anyway.
94	///
95	/// Service-readiness waits are folded into the charge tabtarget itself,
96	/// driven by the per-nameplate `RBRN_BOTTLE_READINESS_DELAY_SEC` fact.
97	pub fn rbtdrc_charge_crucible() -> Result<(), String> {

100	        let ctx = opt
101	            .as_mut()

107	/// Teardown hook for crucible fixtures: quench → inverse CrucibleActive
108	/// assertion. Reads ctx from thread-local. Best-effort — surfaces failures as
109	/// stderr warnings rather than errors; teardown always completes.
110	pub fn rbtdrc_quench_crucible() {
111	    RBTDRC_CTX.with(|c| {
112	        let mut opt = c.borrow_mut();
113	        if let Some(ctx) = opt.as_mut() {

121	fn zrbtdrc_charge_impl(ctx: &mut rbtdri_Context) -> Result<(), String> {

128	        Ok(r) => {

130	        }
131	        Err(e) => {
132	            return Err(format!("Charge invocation failed: {}", e));
133	        }
134	    }
135	
136	    crate::rbtdrg_info_now!("Verifying crucible is active after charge...");
137	    match rbtdri_invoke_global(ctx, RBTDGC_CRUCIBLE_ACTIVE, &[&fixture], &[]) {
138	        Ok(r) if r.exit_code == 0 => {
139	            crate::rbtdrg_info_now!("Crucible active confirmed");
140	        }
141	        Ok(r) => {
142	            return Err(format!(
143	                "Lifecycle assertion failed: crucible not active after charge (exit {})\n{}",

206	    ctx: &mut rbtdri_Context,
207	    attack: &str,
208	    extra_args: &[&str],
209	    dir: &Path,
210	) -> rbtdre_Verdict {

222	/// Execute a command in the sentry via writ, returning captured stdout.
223	///
224	/// Runs under BURD_NO_LOG so the BUK dispatch does not fold the tabtarget's
225	/// stderr into stdout (nor emit log-path headers). The captured stdout is then
226	/// exactly the sentry command's output — rbob_writ's "Writ to sentry" status
227	/// line (buc_step, stderr) stays out of it, so a value-read needs no header or
228	/// ANSI filtering and is independent of terminal color (NO_COLOR, dumb TERM,
229	/// plain ssh pipe). theurge keeps its own per-invocation stdout/stderr capture,
230	/// so dropping the redundant logs-buk transcript for these probe writs costs
231	/// no diagnostics.
232	fn rbtdrc_writ(ctx: &mut rbtdri_Context, args: &[&str]) -> Result<String, String> {
233	    let result = rbtdri_invoke_env(ctx, RBTDGC_CRUCIBLE_WRIT, args, &[("BURD_NO_LOG", "1")])?;
234	    if result.exit_code != 0 {
235	        return Err(format!(
236	            "writ exit {}\nstdout: {}\nstderr: {}",
237	            result.exit_code, result.stdout, result.stderr
238	        ));
239	    }
240	    Ok(result.stdout)
241	}
242	
243	/// Execute a command in the pentacle via fiat, returning the invocation result.
244	fn rbtdrc_fiat(ctx: &mut rbtdri_Context, args: &[&str]) -> Result<String, String> {
245	    let result = rbtdri_invoke(ctx, RBTDGC_CRUCIBLE_FIAT, args)?;
246	    if result.exit_code != 0 {
247	        return Err(format!(
248	            "fiat exit {}\nstdout: {}\nstderr: {}",
249	            result.exit_code, result.stdout, result.stderr
250	        ));
251	    }
252	    Ok(result.stdout)
253	}
254	
255	/// Discover the sentry's enclave IP by reading /etc/resolv.conf from the pentacle via fiat.
256	/// The pentacle uses the sentry as its DNS server, so resolv.conf nameserver = sentry enclave IP.
257	/// (Sentry's own resolv.conf points to upstream DNS like 8.8.8.8 — wrong for enclave ops.)
258	fn rbtdrc_discover_sentry_ip(ctx: &mut rbtdri_Context) -> Result<String, String> {
259	    let output = rbtdrc_fiat(ctx, &["cat", "/etc/resolv.conf"])?;
260	    for line in output.lines() {
261	        let trimmed = line.trim();
262	        if let Some(rest) = trimmed.strip_prefix("nameserver") {
263	            let ip = rest.trim();
264	            if !ip.is_empty() && rbtdrc_looks_like_ip(ip) {
265	                return Ok(ip.to_string());
266	            }
267	        }
268	    }
269	    Err(format!(
270	        "no nameserver found in pentacle /etc/resolv.conf:\n{}",
271	        output
272	    ))
273	}
274	
275	/// Resolve a hostname via writ (running dig +short on the sentry).
276	/// Returns the first line that looks like an IP address (filters BUK log headers).
277	fn rbtdrc_resolve_via_writ(ctx: &mut rbtdri_Context, hostname: &str) -> Result<String, String> {
278	    let output = rbtdrc_writ(ctx, &["dig", "+short", hostname])?;
279	    let ip = output
280	        .lines()
281	        .find(|line| {
282	            let t = line.trim();
283	            !t.is_empty() && rbtdrc_looks_like_ip(t)
284	        })
285	        .map(|line| line.trim().to_string())
286	        .ok_or_else(|| format!("dig +short {} returned no IP:\n{}", hostname, output))?;
287	    Ok(ip)
288	}
289	
290	/// Quick check: does this string look like an IPv4 address (digits and dots only)?
291	/// Not a full validator — just enough to reject BUK log headers and DNS comments.
292	fn rbtdrc_looks_like_ip(s: &str) -> bool {
293	    !s.is_empty() && s.chars().all(|c| c.is_ascii_digit() || c == '.')
294	}
295	
296	/// Extract iptables rule lines from writ output, filtering BUK log headers.
297	/// Rules start with -P (policy), -N (new chain), or -A (append).
298	fn rbtdrc_extract_iptables_rules(output: &str) -> String {
299	    output
300	        .lines()
301	        .filter(|l| {
302	            let t = l.trim();
303	            t.starts_with("-P ") || t.starts_with("-N ") || t.starts_with("-A ")
304	        })
305	        .collect::<Vec<_>>()
306	        .join("\n")
307	}
308	
309	/// Read a named environment variable from sentry's process environment via writ.
310	/// writ runs under BURD_NO_LOG, so its stdout is the clean command output —
311	/// the first non-empty line is the value, with no header/ANSI stripping needed.
312	fn rbtdrc_read_sentry_env(ctx: &mut rbtdri_Context, var: &str) -> Result<String, String> {
313	    let output = rbtdrc_writ(ctx, &["printenv", var])?;
314	    output
315	        .lines()
316	        .map(|l| l.trim())
317	        .find(|l| !l.is_empty())
318	        .map(|l| l.to_string())

459	// Structural-presence backstops for the load-bearing iptables clauses and
460	// kernel sysctl set by rbjs_sentry.sh. Each case is a fast read-and-check
461	// against sentry's runtime state — fail loudly if a future change strips a
462	// defensive clause the architecture depends on.
463	
464	fn rbtdrc_sentry_config_rp_filter(dir: &Path) -> rbtdre_Verdict {
465	    rbtdrc_with_ctx(|ctx| {
466	        let entry_mode = match rbtdrc_read_sentry_env(ctx, "RBRN_ENTRY_MODE") {
467	            Ok(m) => m,
468	            Err(e) => return rbtdre_Verdict::Fail(format!("read RBRN_ENTRY_MODE: {}", e)),
469	        };
470	        let output = match rbtdrc_writ(ctx, &["cat", "/proc/sys/net/ipv4/conf/all/rp_filter"]) {
471	            Ok(o) => o,
472	            Err(e) => return rbtdre_Verdict::Fail(format!("writ error: {}", e)),
473	        };
474	        let _ = std::fs::write(dir.join("rp_filter.txt"), &output);
475	
476	        let value = output
477	            .lines()
478	            .map(|l| l.trim())
479	            .find(|l| !l.is_empty())
480	            .map(|l| l.to_string())
481	            .unwrap_or_default();
482	
483	        let expected = match entry_mode.as_str() {
484	            "rbnne_enabled" => "2",
485	            "rbnne_disabled" => "1",
486	            other => {
487	                return rbtdre_Verdict::Fail(format!("unexpected RBRN_ENTRY_MODE '{}'", other));
488	            }
489	        };
490	
491	        if value != expected {
492	            return rbtdre_Verdict::Fail(format!(
493	                "rp_filter = {} (expected {} for entry-mode {})",
494	                value, expected, entry_mode
495	            ));
496	        }
497	        rbtdre_Verdict::Pass
498	    })
499	}

501	fn rbtdrc_sentry_config_prerouting_dnat(dir: &Path) -> rbtdre_Verdict {
502	    rbtdrc_with_ctx(|ctx| {
503	        let entry_mode = match rbtdrc_read_sentry_env(ctx, "RBRN_ENTRY_MODE") {
504	            Ok(m) => m,
505	            Err(e) => return rbtdre_Verdict::Fail(format!("read RBRN_ENTRY_MODE: {}", e)),
506	        };
507	        if entry_mode != "rbnne_enabled" {
508	            return rbtdre_Verdict::Skip(format!("entry-mode={}", entry_mode));
509	        }
510	        let output = match rbtdrc_writ(ctx, &["iptables", "-t", "nat", "-S", "PREROUTING"]) {
511	            Ok(o) => o,
512	            Err(e) => return rbtdre_Verdict::Fail(format!("writ error: {}", e)),
513	        };
514	        let _ = std::fs::write(dir.join("prerouting.txt"), &output);
515	
516	        let append_rules: Vec<&str> = output
517	            .lines()
518	            .filter(|l| l.trim().starts_with("-A "))
519	            .collect();
520	
521	        let return_positions: Vec<usize> = append_rules
522	            .iter()
523	            .enumerate()
524	            .filter(|(_, r)| r.contains("-j RETURN"))
525	            .map(|(i, _)| i)
526	            .collect();
527	        let dnat_positions: Vec<usize> = append_rules
528	            .iter()
529	            .enumerate()
530	            .filter(|(_, r)| r.contains("-j DNAT"))
531	            .map(|(i, _)| i)
532	            .collect();
533	
534	        if return_positions.len() != 2 {
535	            return rbtdre_Verdict::Fail(format!(
536	                "PREROUTING RETURN rule count = {} (expected 2)",
537	                return_positions.len()
538	            ));
539	        }
540	        if dnat_positions.is_empty() {
541	            return rbtdre_Verdict::Fail("PREROUTING DNAT rule absent".to_string());
542	        }
543	        let max_return = *return_positions.iter().max().unwrap();
544	        let min_dnat = *dnat_positions.iter().min().unwrap();
545	        if max_return >= min_dnat {
546	            return rbtdre_Verdict::Fail(format!(
547	                "PREROUTING RETURN at position {} not before DNAT at position {}",
548	                max_return, min_dnat
549	            ));
550	        }
551	        rbtdre_Verdict::Pass
552	    })
553	}

616	                return rbtdre_Verdict::Fail("FORWARD -j RBM-FORWARD jump absent".to_string());
617	            }
618	        };
619	
620	        if estab_pos >= jump_pos {
621	            return rbtdre_Verdict::Fail(format!(
622	                "FORWARD ESTABLISHED,RELATED at position {} not before RBM-FORWARD jump at position {}",
623	                estab_pos, jump_pos
624	            ));
625	        }
626	        rbtdre_Verdict::Pass
627	    })

630	fn rbtdrc_dns_blocked_with_observation(dir: &Path) -> rbtdre_Verdict {
631	    rbtdrc_with_ctx(|ctx| {
632	        // Before: capture iptables state from sentry
633	        let before = match rbtdrc_writ(ctx, &["iptables", "-L", "-v", "-n", "-x"]) {
634	            Ok(o) => {
635	                let _ = std::fs::write(dir.join("iptables-before.txt"), &o);
636	                o
637	            }
638	            Err(e) => return rbtdre_Verdict::Fail(format!("before-capture: {}", e)),
639	        };
640	
641	        // Attack: invoke ifrit dns-blocked-google inside bottle
642	        let ifrit_verdict = rbtdrc_invoke_ifrit(ctx, "dns-blocked-google", dir);
643	
644	        // After: capture iptables state from sentry
645	        let after = match rbtdrc_writ(ctx, &["iptables", "-L", "-v", "-n", "-x"]) {
646	            Ok(o) => {
647	                let _ = std::fs::write(dir.join("iptables-after.txt"), &o);
648	                o
649	            }
650	            Err(e) => return rbtdre_Verdict::Fail(format!("after-capture: {}", e)),
651	        };
652	
653	        // Log observation: did sentry state change during the attack?
654	        let delta = if before != after {
655	            "CHANGED — sentry processed traffic during attack"
656	        } else {
657	            "UNCHANGED — no observable sentry state change"
658	        };
659	        let observation = format!(
660	            "Ifrit verdict: {}\nIptables delta: {}\n",
661	            match &ifrit_verdict {
662	                rbtdre_Verdict::Pass => "PASS",
663	                rbtdre_Verdict::Fail(_) => "FAIL",
664	                rbtdre_Verdict::Skip(_) => "SKIP",
665	            },
666	            delta,
667	        );
668	        let _ = std::fs::write(dir.join("observation.txt"), &observation);
669	
670	        // Primary verdict is the ifrit result
671	        ifrit_verdict
672	    })
673	}

816	/// Coordinated: attempt outbound connections from sentry itself to non-allowed destinations.

818	fn rbtdrc_coordinated_sentry_egress_lockdown(dir: &Path) -> rbtdre_Verdict {
819	    rbtdrc_with_ctx(|ctx| {
820	        // Negative: attempt TCP to 1.1.1.1:443 from sentry via bash /dev/tcp
821	        let tcp1_result = rbtdri_invoke(
822	            ctx,

824	            &["timeout", "2", "bash", "-c", "echo > /dev/tcp/1.1.1.1/443"],
825	        );
826	        let tcp1_blocked = match &tcp1_result {
827	            Ok(r) => {
828	                let _ = std::fs::write(dir.join("tcp1-stdout.txt"), &r.stdout);
829	                let _ = std::fs::write(dir.join("tcp1-stderr.txt"), &r.stderr);
830	                r.exit_code != 0
831	            }
832	            Err(e) => {
833	                let _ = std::fs::write(dir.join("tcp1-error.txt"), format!("{}", e));
834	                true // invocation error = blocked
835	            }
836	        };
837	        if !tcp1_blocked {
838	            return rbtdre_Verdict::Fail(
839	                "BREACH: sentry TCP to 1.1.1.1:443 succeeded — OUTPUT chain not blocking egress"
840	                    .to_string(),
841	            );
842	        }
843	
844	        // Negative: attempt TCP to 140.82.121.4:443 (GitHub) from sentry
845	        let tcp2_result = rbtdri_invoke(
846	            ctx,
847	            RBTDGC_CRUCIBLE_WRIT,
848	            &[
849	                "timeout",
850	                "2",
851	                "bash",
852	                "-c",
853	                "echo > /dev/tcp/140.82.121.4/443",
854	            ],
855	        );

857	            Ok(r) => {

859	                let _ = std::fs::write(dir.join("tcp2-stderr.txt"), &r.stderr);
860	                r.exit_code != 0
861	            }
862	            Err(e) => {
863	                let _ = std::fs::write(dir.join("tcp2-error.txt"), format!("{}", e));
864	                true
865	            }
866	        };
867	        if !tcp2_blocked {
868	            return rbtdre_Verdict::Fail(
869	                "BREACH: sentry TCP to 140.82.121.4:443 succeeded — OUTPUT chain not blocking egress"
870	                    .to_string(),
871	            );
872	        }
873	
874	        // Positive control: dig @8.8.8.8 connectivity domain from sentry must succeed
875	        // (proves dnsmasq's egress path works — specific OUTPUT ACCEPT for DNS)
876	        let dig_result = match rbtdrc_writ(
877	            ctx,
878	            &["dig", "+short", "@8.8.8.8", RBTDRC_CONNECTIVITY_DOMAIN],
879	        ) {
880	            Ok(o) => o,
881	            Err(e) => {
882	                return rbtdre_Verdict::Fail(format!(
883	                    "positive control failed: dig @8.8.8.8 {} from sentry: {}", RBTDRC_CONNECTIVITY_DOMAIN,
884	                    e
885	                ))
886	            }
887	        };
888	        let _ = std::fs::write(dir.join("dig-positive.txt"), &dig_result);
889	
890	        let has_ip = dig_result
891	            .lines()
892	            .any(|l| rbtdrc_looks_like_ip(l.trim()));
893	        if !has_ip {
894	            return rbtdre_Verdict::Fail(format!(
895	                "positive control: dig @8.8.8.8 {} returned no IP:\n{}", RBTDRC_CONNECTIVITY_DOMAIN,
896	                dig_result
897	            ));
898	        }
899	
900	        let _ = std::fs::write(
901	            dir.join("observation.txt"),
902	            "Sentry egress lockdown verified: TCP to 1.1.1.1:443 blocked, TCP to 140.82.121.4:443 blocked, DNS egress works",
903	        );
904	        rbtdre_Verdict::Pass
905	    })

923	
924	        // Invoke ifrit dns-blocked-google via bark
925	        let blocked_result = match rbtdri_invoke(

934	        let _ = std::fs::write(dir.join("blocked-stderr.txt"), &blocked_result.stderr);
935	
936	        // Brief delay for dnsmasq log flush
937	        std::thread::sleep(std::time::Duration::from_millis(500));

974	        let _ = std::fs::write(

977	        );

35	// only the release kit roots, flagging each command-position token not in {bash
